Overall:
The following processes are effective for the removal of aldicarb: reverse osmosis membrane (equal or greater than 98 percent removal), granular activated carbon (97 percent removal) and ultraviolet irradiation with ozone (83 percent removal).  Performance of these technologies can vary based on dose, contact time and water quality characteristics.
